content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

October 30, 1990

Peter A. Bagatelos
Bagatelos & Fadem
601 California Street, Suite 1801
San Francisco, CA 94108

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-90-561

Dear Mr. Bagatelos:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform
Act (the "Act").1/ Since your request for advice is of a general
nature, we treat your letter as a request for informal assistance
pursuant to the provisions of Regulation 18329(c) (copy
enclosed).2/

QUESTION

How is the fair market value for private air transportation
provided to a candidate determined?

CONCLUSION

The fair market value for private air transportation is
either the commercial air rate for a similar trip or the charter
rate divided by the number of passengers.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

ANALYSIS

The fair market value of private air transportation is
determined by using a commercial air rate, or, alternatively, a
charter rate divided by the number of passengers. (In re Stone
(1977) 3 FEC Ops. 52, copy enclosed.) One of these methods must
be used to determine the value of private air transportation when
the transportation is a gift or an in-kind contribution. (Taylor
Advice Letter, No. A-84-109, and Langston Advice Letter, No. A-90-
041, copies enclosed.) The actual cost to operate a private
aircraft cannot be used to determine the fair market value.

Either a charter rate or a commercial air rate may be applied
when there is established commercial air carrier transportation
between two particular locations. When there is no commercial air
flight, a charter rate must be used.

The fair market value of a commercial air rate is the normal
and customary airfare charged by a particular airline to the
public. When a discounted fare is sold on the open market to the
general public, the fair market value is the discounted price. (In
re Hopkins (1977) 3 FEC Ops. 107, copy enclosed.) When using a
charter rate, the fair market value is determined by dividing the
cost among the total number of passengers on the flight.

Although private air transportation often provides a benefit
of convenience to a candidate or officeholder, a value for the
convenience is not required to be determined in computing the fair
market value.

Please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you
have additional questions.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Lynda Cassady
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosures

The fair market value of private air transportation is determined by using a commercial air rate, or, alternatively, a charter rate divided by the number of passengers. (In re Stone (1977) 3 FPPC Ops. 52, copy enclosed.) One of these methods must be used to determine the value of private air transportation when the transportation is a gift or an in-kind contribution. (Taylor Advice Letter, No. A-84-109, and Langston Advice Letter, No. A-90-041, copies enclosed.) The actual cost to operate a private aircraft cannot be used to determine the fair market value.

The fair market value of a commercial air rate is the normal and customary airfare charged by a particular airline to the public.
